evidence.velocity_vs_rigour: We're looking for someone with strong analytics engineering fundamentals and the ability to apply them pragmatically in a fast-moving environment.
evidence.velocity_vs_rigour_explanation: Multiple velocity phrases ('fast-moving environment', 'move faster', 'growing fast') appear alongside rigour signals (data quality, scalability, maintainability, reliability), giving parity between the two per the tie-breaker.
evidence.domain_risk: Owning and improving the data models that support lending decisions, pricing, portfolio analysis, and investor reporting.
evidence.domain_risk_explanation: The role directly owns data models feeding lending decisions and investor reporting at a fintech lender, matching high domain risk via the default-high fintech sector plus explicit financial-reporting language.
evidence.collaboration_width: analysts; product teams; backend engineers; data platform team
evidence.data_team_maturity: Helping define good modelling patterns, architecture, and implementation standards across the analytics engineering layer.
evidence.data_team_maturity_explanation: Named tools (dbt, Snowflake, Fivetran) appear in responsibilities alongside an existing data platform team and analyst cohort to mentor, indicating a mid-stage, actively growing analytics engineering practice.
evidence.jd_authorship: Owning and improving the data models that support lending decisions, pricing, portfolio analysis, and investor reporting. Driving the development of our dbt models and transformation layer
evidence.jd_authorship_explanation: Responsibilities name specific tools, domains, and precise application context (dbt models, Snowflake, lending decisions, investor reporting), letting the reader understand the actual day-to-day work.
evidence.stakeholder_orientation: Owning and improving the data models that support lending decisions, pricing, portfolio analysis, and investor reporting.
evidence.stakeholder_orientation_explanation: The named outputs (lending decisions, pricing, portfolio analysis, investor reporting) are finance-domain deliverables, matching finance orientation despite cross-functional collaboration.
evidence.autonomy_level: We're looking for a Senior Analytics Engineer to own the analytical foundation for our US Loans team... You'll operate with a high degree of ownership, helping shape the modelling layer
evidence.autonomy_level_explanation: The role explicitly owns the analytical foundation and shapes the modelling layer and architecture standards, matching strategic direction-setting verbs ('own', 'shape', 'define').
evidence.ai_role: Comfort using AI tools effectively to move faster, improve quality, and strengthen day-to-day analytical and engineering workflows.
evidence.ai_role_explanation: The candidate is explicitly expected to use AI tools (including Claude, named in the tech stack) to accelerate their own engineering work, matching the ai_user pattern.
evidence.testing_framing: The role is fundamentally about building a strong analytical foundation... while maintaining high standards around data quality, scalability, and maintainability.
evidence.testing_framing_explanation: Data quality and reliability standards are framed as something the candidate owns and defines across the analytics engineering layer, matching responsibility rather than a listed tool.
evidence.loss_aversion_framing: Identifying opportunities to improve the efficiency, reliability, and cost-effectiveness of our transformation pipeline over time.
evidence.loss_aversion_framing_explanation: Pipeline reliability and data quality are named concerns but secondary to the growth/ownership/delivery framing dominating the JD, and there is no explicit compliance/regulatory register, keeping this moderate.
evidence.greenfield_vs_fix: Scaling our data infrastructure to proactively support the requirements of a rapidly growing business. ... Helping define good modelling patterns, architecture, and implementation standards
evidence.greenfield_vs_fix_explanation: The role both scales existing infrastructure and defines new modelling patterns/architecture standards, giving a mixed greenfield/fix signal.
evidence.work_arrangement: Location Type: Hybrid; Hybrid roles require three days in-office weekly
evidence.loss_aversion: "Identifying opportunities to improve the efficiency, reliability, and cost-effectiveness of our transformation pipeline over time" — reliability is a stated concern but secondary to growth and ownership framing.
